qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: HTTP request smuggling via Transfer-Encoding ignored when Content-Length appears first
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and vulnerable code: this finding concerns HTTP/1.1 Content-Length versus Transfer-Encoding precedence in lib/facil/http/parsers/http1_parser.h, not zero-progress numeral parsing in fio_json_parse/fio_json_parser.h.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: WebSocket NULL pointer dereference via orphan continuation frame crashes server
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and vulnerable code: this finding concerns WebSocket continuation-frame state handling in lib/facil/http/websockets.c, not the JSON parser numeral branch that loops on bare i/I tokens.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding in the run manifest identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory is an infinite loop in fio_json_parse/fio_json_parser.h when bare i/I in nested JSON causes fio_atol to consume zero bytes and leave the parser position unchanged. The manifest qualifying findings cover unrelated HTTP request smuggling and WebSocket NULL dereference denial of service.
evidence: Advisory root cause: lib/facil/fiobj/fio_json_parser.h numeral handling around lines 434-468 accepts bare i/I after fio_atol consumes zero characters, leaving pos unchanged inside nested parsing.
http-request-smuggling/vulnerability_report.json identifies lib/facil/http/parsers/http1_parser.h header precedence logic for Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding.
websocket-null-deref-dos/vulnerability_report.json identifies lib/facil/http/websockets.c continuation-frame handling and fiobj_str_write on FIOBJ_INVALID.
A session search for fio_json_parse, fio_json_parser, fiobj_json2obj, fio_atol, the [i payload, CVE-2026-41146, and GHSA-2x79 found no matching final qualifying finding.
